All right, we are going into the client tracking spreadsheet. It is awesome and it will keep you super organized with all of your clients. So let's dig into this thing. The first thing that you're going to want to notice are the tabs on the bottom. We have client list. We have client tracking. We have tracking by trainer, so trainer A, B, C, and D. Then we have two more tabs, one for the people that canceled and one for your attrition. So that is what this spreadsheet is tracking. These are all of your EFTs, all of your regular clients. So let's go to the first page, first tab, which would be client list. And here you want to, the only thing you have to update is your clients. That's all. You can put your trainer names here if you'd like to. Instead of having trainer A, B, C, D, you can put their names if you're going to be assigning them or you can just leave them A, B, C, D. It's up to you. But this is going to be a quick shot of how many clients you have. So anytime you add an EFT or you lose an EFT, make sure you get rid of them on this list. So this, you can scroll down and say, just easily see, I have 102 clients without doing any math, anything like that. It's just right here. This is where you keep them all. This month, you don't have to change. It just, we're using it in the formulas elsewhere. So that's it. And when you go to client tracking, let's start on the left-hand side and we'll move our way right. So the first top cell you'll want to notice is the guide or the key for the colors. So gray will be all of your online clients. Green would be large group. Blue would be high ticket or red being the clients that canceled. And instead of deleting them off the sheet, when they're canceled, you want to keep them on there so you can get an idea of your lifetime value and essentially how long it's taking to, for clients to cancel. And if you see any trends, like maybe everybody's canceling like month four, then month three, you want to do reach outs and extra things like that. But for the most part, we're going to be using these four colors to track all of it. So now we are looking underneath and this is going to, you're going to have your challenge groups separated here because all of these are going to be the people that are moving right to the right hand. You can copy these ones right here and move them right over to your next month once they become EFTs. Or if they don't, you can move them over to challenged. I'm sorry, canceled. So here we have five, six groups. You have never been to challenge groups you have going at once. And you can just name the groups that basically, if they started the first week of January, you can call them January group one, second week of January group two, three, four, right? Just to keep it super simple. And then we have the names in there. And you are tracking their journey to see how long they stay with you and what kind of members they are. So that is all we're doing here. Then you can move to the right where we have the canceled clients. So this is challengers that exited. So when you drop down, you'll be able to find the challenger names. And this is for challengers that did not make it over to EFT. So you want to make sure that you are, let's say, now here didn't, they didn't make it over to EFT in this column over to the right. They decided not to continue after the challenge. So we're going to put now over here and we are going to turn that red just to show us and we're going to delete it off of here. That's it. Okay. So pretty simple. Then all you can do is you can over here, once the week is over, change this to January group two and you can copy these names and you can put them up here. And now we just moved. You can put a new title here. But we just moved it over and now they're the next group in the hopper that are about to finish. So that's how you do the right here is where we have our clients broken up to trainers. Okay. So ABCD for blocks of trainers. And again, you can just toggle down. And right now I have all of the, you know, trainer A has all the online clients, trainer B has all the high ticket, trainer C has all the large group. You totally don't have to do it like that. In fact, you like trainer D has a mix of them. You can have a mix of them. However you want to set it up, but this is just for you to know. Okay. So when, you know, when Tanner here, when he gets moved over and he gets added as an EFT, don't forget you have to assign them to somebody. So I'll put Tanner over here and now Tanner is has been assigned to trainer B. So then the next thing that we want to do is after we have moved them from challenger to EFT and then assign their trainer, don't forget to add their trainer to, or I'm sorry, their their name to the trainer's tracker, their daily tracker. So let's go into that sheet now. Let's go to trainer B and this is the tracker. So we need to add Tanner because we just, there he is, A to make an attempt. Okay. So as an example, we're going to show you a method on how to automatically send them out a text every two weeks, every week, however frequently you want to. But if they're not texting you back, if you're not getting any responses, then you want to send like a purposeful like text and then check off attempt one. Right. So let's say Venus has not texted us back. We texted her out at one five, but I'm sorry, two five, but the last time she texted back was one one. Okay. So it'll automatically update the numbers. So it's like, wow, that's, that's been a, that's been a long time. It's been a month and a half almost. She hasn't got back to me. So I'm going to do a checkbox of attempt one and all that means is that you're going to say like, Hey, is everything okay? Like it's not your normal text. It's like, Hey, is everything okay? I haven't heard from you in a month. Like I just want to make sure like I'm your trainer. You know what I mean? Like I want to make sure everything's going good. So then you would just put that in the note attempted on two five. So then that's your, that's your last out would be two five and you attempted, let's say she still doesn't get back to you. Then you can start adding new accountability coaches or trainers once you get more clients than like 200 or 300 or 400 something like that. So again, this is how we're using these. It's all pretty simple and you want to divide and conquer and break your clients some or break your clients up amongst your trainers. So the next piece that we are going to go into is cancels. So here we are cancels and we have again a dropdown where you can choose the name. And what I like to do is as soon as somebody cancels, I will put, I'll find their name. So let's say it's, you know, uh, whoops, Mercedes, I will put her here. And then I am going to go to the client list and I'm going to find Mercedes. Mercedes. There she is. And I'm going to delete her. Once I delete her, I can just reorganize this and I like to keep it in alphabetical order. That's just me. Whoops. I'll tie these headings up to the top so they don't move. But then it, uh, when this person is all said and done, they're done at your gym. All the things have been processed. You check this off and that's it. So that's how you track cancellations. So they don't just get left by the wayside and you make sure that you did the entire process. So that is that sheet. And then the final sheet is attrition. Okay. This sheet is basically tracking how many people you have lost and how many people you have gained and what your net number of clients are. So to make sure that you always know what your net number of clients are, keep in mind, this number here, which is the current month that we're in in this example, um, 364 should always match the number of clients that you have on your list here. Right. So when you scroll down and you get down to the bottom, it should say, well, it would say three 20, three 64. If they're off and somebody's missing, you're going to want to take a closer look at it. Okay. So back over here, um, all you are doing is entering the people that canceled from that month. So you'll go back to your cancel and you'll say, okay, from this month we have one, two, three, uh, three people that canceled. So you go over here and you put in three. Okay. So when you put in three, it's going to tell you what your, um, attrition is. Okay. So, um, then you're going to want to enter in how many people you signed up. So you go back to your client tracking sheet and it's like, okay, well, you know, let's just use this March as an example. One, two, three, four, five, six, seven. So we signed up seven people. Let's put that in there. Pretty cool. So we netted four. Four was our net, um, clients. And then we moved this over, uh, to the right. It automatically populates it. So now we are at 368, uh, percent. So, um, we have it, these, uh, I'll have to update this with the, uh, the coloring, um, just for this one, but they should all look the lower, the lower your attrition, the greener it is, the higher your attrition, the more red it is. And that's just a cool indicator to kind of let you know where it's at. Um, and we'll just update it up here. Again, you'll want to look at the average attrition over the last three months. So, um, if it's getting better, if your trends are getting better, it's going to show, because you could have an off month and it's just an off month, but the six month rolling is really, um, on average, how well you do. So this attrition sheet always tells you where you're at for attrition wise. Um, and it also tells you, uh, your, your total for the month. Okay. So I'm, for the year. So, so far, um, we've gained 34. We have lost 19. So our average attrition, um, is pretty low. It's at 0.44%. I think that's off a little bit. Yeah, I'll recalculate. Oh no, it is off because it's calculating all of these zeros in there as well. So I'll update that. But, um, pretty simple and you're just going to want to find the year and the month that you are in and keep this super updated so you know what your attrition is and make sure that your total numbers up here are accurate with how many people are on your list. So that is how this entire spreadsheet works. It's very simple. It's all manual processed, but it'll keep you super organized. Okay. So now that we've kind of explained the tracking sheet that we're going to be using, we can go into the next piece, which will, um, go over the, the processes that we're using, um, with all of our clients.
